key_insight: A transfer-learned, uncertainty-aware transformer can screen and rank greener solvent substitutes from sparse data by predicting key solubility descriptors with high accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster solvent selection for materials manufacturing and discovery workflows in photovoltaics, batteries, and catalysis while reducing reliance on trial-and-error experimentation.
method: Pre-train on QM9 properties, then transfer to solvent-relevant targets with minimal labeled data.
Add uncertainty quantification so users can assess confidence before acting on predictions.
Deploy a screening tool that ranks candidate solvents for green substitution.
evidence_signal: The model performs well on well-studied targets like Hansen solubility parameters and dielectric constant, and also on data-scarce targets such as Gutmann donor and acceptor numbers, while rediscovering known green solvents and proposing new ones.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the limited target datasets, so uncertain predictions should be treated cautiously in high-stakes solvent replacement decisions.
paper_title: A green solvent screening tool for emerging materials via uncertainty aware, transformer enhanced transfer learning
